page: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 192 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by AMD processors, operating with a shared CPU allocation model and a single thread per core. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

Storage is bundled directly with the instance, consisting of 11520 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity. This high-capacity local storage is designed for data-intensive operations requiring local read and write access. The network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is included in this server configuration.

The shared CPU allocation model offers a distinct cost-efficiency profile, trading dedicated CPU execution time for a lower resource-to-cost ratio at a massive scale of 192 cores. This makes the instance suitable for workloads that require high memory capacity and substantial local storage but do not demand continuous, dedicated 100% CPU utilization. Appropriate workloads include large-scale web serving, distributed databases, batch processing, and general-purpose applications with variable CPU demands.
description: Vultr vx1-g-192c-768g-11520s is an x86_64 server instance in the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 192 AMD vCPUs and 192 physical cores under a shared CPU allocation model. It is configured with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes 11520 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address, with no GPU accelerators. The shared CPU architecture provides a cost-efficient option for high-density resource requirements where continuous dedicated CPU performance is not mandatory. This server is designed for large-scale general-purpose workloads, distributed databases, and batch processing applications that benefit from massive local storage and memory capacity.

tagline: A high-capacity shared-core instance featuring 192 AMD vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 11.5 TB of local NVMe SSD storage.
